delta.text: Now I understand the issue. The parser's `cg_exp_to_next_reg`/`cg_exp_to_reg` don't hand
le `ExprKind::Jmp` (produced by comparisons like `1==
1`) or the `e.t != e.f` jumplist case from `and`/`or` short-circuits. I'll restructure these to mirror C's `disc
harge2reg` + `exp2reg` properly, adding the missing helpers.

content.text: Now I understand the issue. The parser's `cg_exp_to_next_reg`/`cg_exp_to_reg` don't handle `ExprKind::Jmp` (produced by comparisons like `1==1`) or the `e.t != e.f` jumplist case from `and`/`or` short-circuits. I'll restructure these to mirror C's `discharge2reg` + `exp2reg` properly, adding the missing helpers.

delta.text: No

errors — only pre-existing warnings. Build is green.

**Root cause.
** `cg_exp_to_next_reg` and `cg_exp_to_reg` in `crates/l
ua-parse/src/lib.rs` (lines ~1519–1731) lacked the `ExprKind::Jmp` and
jumplist (`e.t != e.f`) cases. After `1==1` produ
ces a `Jmp` expression and the chained `and` accumulates `e.f` via `cg_go_if_true`, the final
expression handed to `cg_exp_to_next_reg` was a `Jmp` with a non-empty false

-jump list — the match's `_ =>` arm raised the "cannot discharge Jmp" error.

**Fix.** Refact
ored the two functions to mirror C's `discharge2reg` + `exp2reg` from `lc

ode.c`:
- New `cg_discharge_to_reg` handles all literal/Reloc/NonReloc for

ms and is a no-op for `Jmp`.
- New `cg_exp_to_reg` calls discharge2reg, then for `Jmp` conc
ats the trailing jump into `e.t`, and when `e.t != e.f` emits the `LFalseS
kip`/`LoadTrue` pair (only when a controller isn't a `TES
TSET`) and patches both lists via new helpers `cg_need_value`, `cg_code_loadb

ool`, `cg_patch_list_aux`.
- `cg_exp_to_next_reg` now just discharges vars, fr

ees the temp, reserves a reg, and delegates.

**State after fix.** `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean (only p
re-existing warnings). Per the task constraints I did not re-run the binary; the mega-loop will re-test.

content.text:
No errors — only pre-existing warnings. Build is green.

**Root cause.** `cg_exp_to_next_reg` and `cg_exp_to_reg` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` (lines ~1519–1731) lacked the `ExprKind::Jmp` and jumplist (`e.t != e.f`) cases. After `1==1` produces a `Jmp` expression and the chained `and` accumulates `e.f` via `cg_go_if_true`, the final expression handed to `cg_exp_to_next_reg` was a `Jmp` with a non-empty false-jump list — the match's `_ =>` arm raised the "cannot discharge Jmp" error.

**Fix.** Refactored the two functions to mirror C's `discharge2reg` + `exp2reg` from `lcode.c`:
- New `cg_discharge_to_reg` handles all literal/Reloc/NonReloc forms and is a no-op for `Jmp`.
- New `cg_exp_to_reg` calls discharge2reg, then for `Jmp` concats the trailing jump into `e.t`, and when `e.t != e.f` emits the `LFalseSkip`/`LoadTrue` pair (only when a controller isn't a `TESTSET`) and patches both lists via new helpers `cg_need_value`, `cg_code_loadbool`, `cg_patch_list_aux`.
- `cg_exp_to_next_reg` now just discharges vars, frees the temp, reserves a reg, and delegates.

**State after fix.** `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean (only pre-existing warnings). Per the task constraints I did not re-run the binary; the mega-loop will re-test.

result:
No errors — only pre-existing warnings. Build is green.

**Root cause.** `cg_exp_to_next_reg` and `cg_exp_to_reg` in `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` (lines ~1519–1731) lacked the `ExprKind::Jmp` and jumplist (`e.t != e.f`) cases. After `1==1` produces a `Jmp` expression and the chained `and` accumulates `e.f` via `cg_go_if_true`, the final expression handed to `cg_exp_to_next_reg` was a `Jmp` with a non-empty false-jump list — the match's `_ =>` arm raised the "cannot discharge Jmp" error.

**Fix.** Refactored the two functions to mirror C's `discharge2reg` + `exp2reg` from `lcode.c`:
- New `cg_discharge_to_reg` handles all literal/Reloc/NonReloc forms and is a no-op for `Jmp`.
- New `cg_exp_to_reg` calls discharge2reg, then for `Jmp` concats the trailing jump into `e.t`, and when `e.t != e.f` emits the `LFalseSkip`/`LoadTrue` pair (only when a controller isn't a `TESTSET`) and patches both lists via new helpers `cg_need_value`, `cg_code_loadbool`, `cg_patch_list_aux`.
- `cg_exp_to_next_reg` now just discharges vars, frees the temp, reserves a reg, and delegates.

**State after fix.** `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is clean (only pre-existing warnings). Per the task constraints I did not re-run the binary; the mega-loop will re-test.
